title: Typo correction + 'did you mean' for unknown flags; scope flag errors to the subcommand
summary: The #1 gap (4/20 intent-corpus entries are dead-end useless_error, ALL unknown-flag cases: --liimt, --jsno, --lim abbreviation, --show_token). argparse emits 'unrecognized arguments: --liimt 5' with the TOP-LEVEL usage and no suggestion, so a single flag typo wedges the agent with no learning signal.
diff_sketch: Two parts, ship the cheap one first. (a) CHEAP: wrap parse_known_args; on leftover unrecognized optionals, compute Levenshtein-1 against the union of known flags and print 'unknown flag --liimt; did you mean --limit?' to stderr. (b) HARDER (peer-flagged): argparse bubbles unrecognized OPTIONALS up to the TOP parser after the subparser returns, so re-attributing them to the chosen subcommand and printing that subcommand's usage requires matching the leftovers against the selected subparser's actions — do this second. Keep allow_abbrev=False; the distance-1 hint also covers abbreviations (--lim→--limit).
risk: Additive: only previously-erroring inputs change. No existing test pins unrecognized-argument behavior (peer-verified), so a 'did you mean' addition won't fight tests. Pin KNOWN_FLAGS to the parser so it can't drift.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-001__flag_typo_hint.test.sh — assert `caption search foo --liimt 5` stderr matches 'did you mean --limit' (part a) and, once part b lands, shows `caption search` usage not top-level.

priority_components.blast_radius_reason: workflow-class: loud (exit 2) but every flag on every command is exposed; agent must consult -h to recover

counter_example: CE: bare 'unrecognized arguments' with no nearest-match

title: Define and document an exit-code dictionary; stop overloading exit 1
summary: Exit 1 is overloaded across missing-env, HTTP 4xx/5xx, Meili-auth, file-read and DB-not-found errors, while semantically-identical bad input splits between exit 1 (hand-validation like --limit<1) and exit 2 (argparse). An agent cannot branch on $? to tell user-fixable from transient-upstream errors.
diff_sketch: Give CliError subclasses distinct exit codes: 1=user-input, 2=usage (argparse, already), 3=missing-config/env, 4=upstream/HTTP, 5=not-found. Route _require_* to code 3, HTTP/Meili failures to 4, 404s to 5. Move the --limit<1 / --index-empty validations to argparse-time so usage errors are uniformly 2. Publish the dictionary in the --help epilog and in capabilities --json.
risk: Changing an exit code is a contract change for any caller that hardcodes 1; mitigate by documenting + shipping the dictionary together and noting it in CHANGELOG. Keep 0/2 semantics stable.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-002__exit_code_dictionary.test.sh — assert missing-env→3, bad HTTP→4, 404→5, --limit 0→2; assert capabilities lists the dictionary.

priority_components.blast_radius_reason: workflow-class: agents retry blindly without categorized exit codes

title: Add `caption capabilities --json` (machine-readable contract)
summary: No capabilities surface exists, so an agent must memorize the contract or re-read --help every session. The CommandSpec table (_command_specs()) already carries name/help/usage/notes/example/default_output/needs_* — a capabilities surface is nearly free to generate from it.
diff_sketch: Add a `capabilities` subcommand that emits JSON: {tool, version (from importlib.metadata), contract_version, commands:[{name,usage,default_output,needs_api,needs_meili,flags:[...],notes,example}], exit_codes:{...}, env_vars:[{name,required_for,documented}]}. Generate the command list directly from _command_specs(); no duplicate source of truth.
risk: Purely additive (new read-only subcommand). Pin the schema with a contract test so it can't silently drift.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-003__capabilities_schema.test.sh — assert `caption capabilities --json` parses, has tool/version/commands/exit_codes/env_vars, and command count == len(_command_specs()).

priority_components.blast_radius_reason: workflow-class: every agent session re-discovers the contract

title: Make `doctor` emit `--output json` and never silent-fail [peer's #1: most insidious]
summary: doctor is special-cased in run() (L655) so --output json is ignored — it always prints custom plain text. On probe failure (no creds / backend down) it prints an EMPTY feature list, exit 0, nothing on stderr — an Axiom-14 silent failure indistinguishable from a healthy tool. Peer-claude argued this should LEAD the apply order: doctor is the only discovery surface, so a green light when the backend is unreachable sends an agent confidently down a broken path (the worst failure class), whereas the flag-typo (R-001) at least fails loudly.
diff_sketch: Stop special-casing doctor in run(); return a dict {organization, features:[...], probes:[{name, available, reason}]} and render via emit_output so --output json works (keep a plain default renderer). Populate `reason` for failed probes (missing CAPTION_API_URL / HTTP 401 / connection error). Add `--strict` to exit non-zero when an expected probe fails.
risk: Plain-text default output should stay byte-compatible for humans; JSON is opt-in via --output json. --strict default off preserves current exit behavior.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-004__doctor_json_and_reasons.test.sh — assert `--output json doctor` is valid JSON with probes[].reason populated on failure; assert `doctor --strict` exits non-zero when a probe fails.

priority_components.blast_radius_reason: silent-fail class: exit-0 'healthy' on a broken backend misleads the agent down a confidently-wrong path — maximal blast radius despite lower frequency than R-001

counter_example: CE: command fails but exits 0 with empty stdout

title: Make dropped data recoverable (--full) AND signal lossiness on stderr
summary: Lossiness is in TWO places (peer-corrected). (1) Renderer: table truncates to 72 chars + dedupes search by projectId; md strips timestamps — recoverable via --output json / --show-dupes / --timestamp. (2) COMMAND LAYER: `_project_view`/`_folder_view` (7/6 fields), `_condense_list` (list_md/list_matters), and especially `create_md`'s unconditional 100-char clip of raw_markdown/plain_text drop data EVEN IN `--output json`, with no escape flag. So the agent's usual escape hatch silently fails for 5 verbs, and create_md can't return its own content at all.
diff_sketch: (a) Add a `--full` (raw, no-condense) flag to list_projects/list_folders/list_md/list_matters and create_md so `--output json --full` returns the complete server record; make create_md's truncation apply only to the human/table view, never to JSON. (b) When a render truncates/dedupes/strips OR a condensed view drops fields, print a one-line stderr note: 'fields condensed; use --output json --full for the complete record'. Don't change defaults (breaking for humans).
risk: (b) is stderr-only, zero risk. (a) is additive (new flag); only create_md's JSON-truncation behavior changes, which is a fidelity FIX an agent depends on.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-005__full_fidelity.test.sh — assert `create_md … --output json --full` returns raw_markdown longer than 100 chars; assert list_projects --full json includes a field outside the 7-field view.

priority_components.blast_radius_reason: silent-data-loss class: --output json looks like the safe choice but still drops fields; loss propagates downstream undetected

counter_example: CE: lossy 'structured' output with no escape hatch

title: Bare `caption` prints the cheat-sheet help (exit 0) instead of an argparse error
summary: Bare `caption` errors with 'the following arguments are required: command' (exit 2) and the short usage — it does not show the rich epilog cheat-sheet. First-try-inevitability says the no-arg invocation should orient the agent.
diff_sketch: Make the subparser not strictly required; in run(), if args.command is None, print the full help (parser.print_help, which includes the epilog cheat-sheet) and exit 0.
risk: Behavior change on bare invocation only (exit 2→0, error→help). Some scripts may assert non-zero on bare; document it.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-006__bare_invocation_help.test.sh — assert bare `caption` exits 0 and stdout contains 'Command Cheat Sheet'.

priority_components.blast_radius_reason: convenience-class: agent recovers via --help in one step today

counter_example: CE: bare invocation errors instead of orienting

title: Add `caption robot-docs guide` — a paste-ready agent handbook in-tool
summary: There is no in-tool agent handbook; an agent must infer conventions (per-command output defaults, the --output json lever, auth resolution order, the sync --test dry-run, exit codes) from scattered --help text.
diff_sketch: Add a `robot-docs guide` subcommand printing a compact markdown handbook: output-default table per command, 'always pass --output json to parse', exit-code dictionary, env-var map, auth-resolution order, sync --test dry-run, capabilities pointer. Render from the same CommandSpec data where possible.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-007__robot_docs_guide.test.sh — assert `caption robot-docs guide` exits 0 and mentions --output json + capabilities + exit codes.

priority_components.blast_radius_reason: workflow-class: external doc lookup per session

title: Add `--dry-run` to create_*/edit_* and guard `sync --session-id '*'`
summary: sync has a clean --test dry-run, but create_project/create_folder/edit_project/edit_folder mutate with no preview. `sync --session-id '*'` sends ALL sessions in one call with no confirmation and no error naming the safer scoped form.
diff_sketch: Add `--dry-run` to create_*/edit_* that builds + prints the request body (method, path, json) and exits 0 without sending — mirroring sync --test. For `sync --session-id '*'` without --test, print a stderr notice of how many sessions will be sent and require --yes (or keep but warn).
risk: --dry-run additive. The sync '*' --yes gate is a mild behavior change for an existing power path; default to warn-not-block to avoid breakage, or gate behind --yes with a clear message.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-008__mutating_dry_run.test.sh — assert `edit_project x --name y --dry-run` prints the PATCH body and makes no HTTP call (transport assertion).

priority_components.blast_radius_reason: workflow-class: reversible mutations, but '*' fan-out has real blast radius

counter_example: CE: mutating op with no preview

title: Add an AGENT section to --help AND inject CommandSpec notes/examples into each subcommand's -h
summary: Two gaps. (1) --help (good cheat-sheet/examples/env block) has no pointer to the structured surface (--output json, per-command output defaults, capabilities, robot-docs, exit codes). (2) Peer-flagged: the genuinely useful CommandSpec.notes/example content appears ONLY in the top-level epilog — `caption search -h` shows bare argparse help. An agent that drills into a subcommand for detail gets LESS than the index page.
diff_sketch: (a) Append an 'AGENT / AUTOMATION' block to the epilog: 'machine use → --output json; `caption capabilities --json`; `caption robot-docs guide`. Output defaults: search/list_*/list_matters=table, dl_transcript/get_md=md, others=json. Exit codes: 0 ok,1 user-input,2 usage,3 config,4 upstream,5 not-found.' (b) In build_parser, pass each spec's notes+example into its subparser (description/epilog) so `caption <cmd> -h` carries the same detail the index does — the data already exists on CommandSpec.
risk: Doc-only; no behavior change. Pin with the existing help-snapshot test style.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-009__help_agent_and_subcmd.test.sh — assert top-level --help contains 'AGENT' + '--output json', and `caption search -h` contains the search example/notes.
counter_example: CE: structured surface + per-cmd detail undiscoverable

title: Unify the CLERK_API_KEY surface across both backends; document AGENT_VIEWER_DATA_DIR
summary: Peer-deepened: the same credential has TWO names, TWO precedences, and TWO error strings depending on the verb. Main-API verbs (token/search/create_*/edit_*/dl_transcript) read CLERK_API_KEY from env ONLY — there is no --clerk-api-key flag (cli.py:644) — and error 'Missing API bearer token. Set CLERK_API_KEY'. Hosted-history verbs (sync/*_md/list_matters) accept BOTH --clerk-api-key and env, and error 'Missing Clerk API key (--clerk-api-key or CLERK_API_KEY)'. An agent that learns --clerk-api-key from sync cannot use it on create_project. Separately, AGENT_VIEWER_DATA_DIR is README-only (not in --help).
diff_sketch: Either add a global --clerk-api-key (and --org-id) so the credential is supplied the same way everywhere, OR document the asymmetry explicitly and unify the two error strings to one phrasing that names the flag (where available) + the env var. Add AGENT_VIEWER_DATA_DIR to the _top_level_help_epilog Environment block.
risk: Adding a global flag is additive; unifying messages touches tests that pin the history-path phrasing — update in lockstep.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-010__auth_consistency.test.sh — assert the same --clerk-api-key path works on a main-API verb, the two error strings match, and --help lists AGENT_VIEWER_DATA_DIR.

priority_components.blast_radius_reason: workflow-class: agent re-learns auth per verb-family; flag learned on one family fails on another

counter_example: CE: same credential, two contradictory surfaces

title: Move the `--output-file` 'Saved …' status line from stdout to stderr
summary: With --output-file, cli.py L670 prints 'Saved <cmd> output to PATH' to STDOUT — a human status line on the data channel. Impact is small (the agent isn't piping when writing a file) but it violates the stdout=data / stderr=diagnostics split.
diff_sketch: In run(), print the write_output_file status message to sys.stderr instead of stdout.
risk: Tiny; only affects where the status line goes. A test asserting it on stdout would need updating.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-011__output_file_status_stderr.test.sh — assert the 'Saved …' line goes to stderr and stdout is empty for --output-file runs.
counter_example: CE: status text on the data channel
notes: Cheap; bundle with R-005 (same channel-hygiene commit) since with --output-file this 'Saved …' line is currently the ONLY thing on stdout.

title: Stop reporting pagination the tool doesn't perform (list_projects / list_folders)
summary: Peer-surfaced (HIGH). `fetch_workspace_items_page` (core.py:229) accepts page+limit and IGNORES BOTH; `_command_list_workspace_items` calls it once and hardcodes `count/totalCount/totalPages: 1` (commands.py:131-137); the spec note claims it 'paginates projects' (cli.py:476). So these verbs silently return only the server's first page while telling the agent (via totalPages:1) that it has everything. An agent that paginates on totalPages stops early and believes its dataset is complete — a determinism/completeness defect that misleads as confidently as a silent-fail.
diff_sketch: Either (a) implement real pagination (loop pages until exhausted, or thread --limit/--cursor through fetch_workspace_items_page and the GET params), OR (b) if the API truly returns everything in one call, drop the misleading totalPages/totalCount fields and the 'paginates' claim and document the single-shot behavior. Do NOT advertise pagination you don't do.
risk: (a) is feature work — coordinate with the maintainer; (b) is a small contract clarification. Either way, the current state actively lies and should not ship. Note: this overlaps a pure correctness bug, but it is included as a rec because the misleading metadata is an AGENT-ERGONOMICS trust failure, not just a bug.
test_plan: regression_tests/R-012__pagination_honesty.test.sh — with a mocked >1-page workspace, assert list_projects returns ALL items (option a) OR omits totalPages and the help no longer says 'paginates' (option b).

priority_components.blast_radius_reason: silent-incompleteness class: agent trusts totalPages:1 and stops, missing data with no signal

counter_example: CE: reports complete when it isn't
